channel.title: Iro AI Blog
channel.description: Short, practical writing on AI fluency, prompting, AI tools, and the active-practice method that beats endless video courses.

item.title: How to actually learn ChatGPT in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Most people learn ChatGPT by random experimentation and hit a ceiling. Here's the active-practice method that builds judgment instead of just memorising prompts.

item.title: The 7 prompt patterns that work everywhere
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Forget magic phrases. These 7 prompt patterns transfer across models and survive model upgrades — the foundation of practical prompt engineering.

item.title: How to spot AI hallucinations in 5 seconds
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI models confidently invent facts, citations, and numbers. Here are the five-second checks that catch most hallucinations — and a longer set for when stakes are high.

item.title: AI agents, explained without the jargon
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Strip away the hype: an AI agent is a system that pursues a goal through multiple steps, often using tools. Here's what they really do, when they're worth using, and where they predictably break.

item.title: The best free AI tools for daily work in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A 2026-up-to-date guide to free-tier AI tools that are actually worth using for daily work — what each is good for, where they fall short, and how to combine them.

item.title: AI for beginners: a 30-day learning plan
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A beginner-friendly 30-day plan to learn AI from scratch: what to practise each week, which free tools to use, and how to build a daily habit that actually sticks.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini in 2026: what each is genuinely best at, where each falls short, and how to pick the right one for writing, research, coding, and everyday work.

item.title: Why your AI prompts aren't working (and how to fix them)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: If ChatGPT keeps giving you generic, useless answers, the prompt is usually the problem. Here are the five most common prompt mistakes and how to fix each one fast.

item.title: AI automation for beginners: 5 things to automate this week
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A beginner's guide to AI automation: five high-value, no-code tasks you can automate this week, plus how to tell when a simple workflow beats a fancy AI agent.

item.title: Iro AI vs Coursiv: which AI learning app is right for you in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Coursiv comparison for 2026: how the two AI learning apps differ on teaching style, pricing, free trials, and who each one is actually best for.

item.title: Iro AI vs Learnova: which AI learning app should you pick in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Learnova comparison for 2026: how the two AI learning apps differ on teaching style, hands-on practice, pricing, and who each one suits best.

item.title: Iro AI vs Brilliant: which should you use to actually get good at AI?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Brilliant comparison for 2026: Brilliant teaches the math and concepts behind AI; Iro AI teaches you to use AI tools for real work. Which fits your goal.

item.title: Iro AI vs Duolingo: the "Duolingo for AI," explained
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Iro AI vs Duolingo in 2026: how the gamified-learning method that made Duolingo famous applies to learning AI tools — and why they're not actually competitors.

item.title: What is AI fluency? A practical definition (and how to build it)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI fluency is the practical ability to use, direct, and evaluate AI tools to get better results. Here is a clear definition, a 4-layer framework, and how to build it in 5 minutes a day.

item.title: What is prompt engineering? A clear definition with examples
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Prompt engineering is the skill of structuring AI instructions to get reliably better output. A plain-English definition, why it works, before-and-after examples, and whether it still matters in 2026.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Claude: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Claude in 2026: an honest, side-by-side comparison of strengths, pricing, and who each is best for — so you can pick the right AI assistant (or use both well).

item.title: How to use AI for studying (without cheating yourself)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use AI for studying the right way: the 5 best ways to study with ChatGPT or Claude, what to avoid so you actually learn, and copy-paste study prompts.

item.title: How to learn AI without coding (a no-code path for 2026)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: You don't need to code to learn AI. Here's the no-code path to AI fluency in 2026 — the tools, the skills, and a five-minute-a-day routine that works for non-technical people.

item.title: What is an LLM? Large language models explained simply
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is an LLM? A large language model is an AI trained on text to predict the next word. Here's how LLMs work, examples, what they're bad at, and how to use them well — in plain English.

item.title: How long does it take to learn AI? A realistic timeline
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How long does it take to learn AI? With five minutes of daily practice, you can be useful in two to three weeks. Here's a realistic week-by-week timeline and how to learn AI faster.

item.title: The 7 AI skills worth learning in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The 7 AI skills worth learning in 2026 — prompting, tool choice, judgment, verification, workflow, agents, and continuous learning — why they're durable, and how to build each one.

item.title: 15 ChatGPT prompts for beginners (copy, paste, and learn)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: 15 ChatGPT prompts for beginners to copy and paste — for work, learning, and everyday life — plus the simple structure that makes any prompt better. No experience needed.
